form.label: Multivariate LSTM Regressor
form.description: On this dashboard there is an example of how a multivariate Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network can be used to predict AC power on an example dataset from the MLTK. Select the number of epochs you want the network to be trained for and also select the number of steps (records) that will be used to make each prediction.

search.query: | inputlookup server_power.csv | streamstats count | eventstats max(count) as records | eval train_split=round(records*0.7) | appendpipe [| where count<train_split | fit AITKContainer algo=multivariate_lstm "ac_power" from "total-unhalted_core_cycles" "total-instructions_retired" "total-last_level_cache_references" "total-memory_bus_transactions" "total-cpu-utilization" "total-disk-accesses" "total-disk-blocks" "total-disk-utilization" epochs=$epochs$ batch_size=$batch_size$ previous_steps=$lookback$ into app:lstm_ac_power] | where count>=train_split AND isnull(predicted_ac_power) | fields - predicted_ac_power | apply lstm_ac_power
| table * | where count>(train_split+1+$lookback$) | table _time ac_power predicted_ac_power
| table * | where count>(train_split+1+$lookback$) | table _time ac_power predicted_ac_power | eval residual=ac_power-predicted_ac_power | `histogram(residual,500)`
| table * | where count>(train_split+1+$lookback$) | table ac_power predicted_ac_power
| table * | where count>(train_split+1+$lookback$) | table _time ac_power predicted_ac_power | score r2_score ac_power against predicted_ac_power

| table * | where count>(train_split+1+$lookback$) | table _time ac_power predicted_ac_power | score mean_squared_error ac_power against predicted_ac_power | eval rmse=sqrt(mean_squared_error) | table rmse
